home.title: DarkTunes Charts
home.subtitle: The fairest dark music charts in the world
home.description: Powered by mathematically proven voting algorithms that eliminate strategic manipulation.

charts.title: Charts
charts.description: Aggregated results from fan voting, DJ ballots, and peer reviews

combinedHelp.title: How are Combined Charts calculated?
combinedHelp.description:
The Combined Charts aggregate three independent voting systems with equal weights (33.3% each):

• Fan Score: Quadratic Voting – 100 credits per month
• DJ Score: Schulze Method – Ranked-Choice Ballots
• Peer Score: Band Peer Review – Anti-collusion algorithm

Each score is Min-Max normalised before combination, ensuring no group can dominate.

tierHelp.title: The Tier System explained
tierHelp.description:
Tiers are based on monthly Spotify stream counts:

• MAJOR: > 1,000,000 streams/month
• INDIE: 100,000 – 1,000,000 streams/month
• MICRO: 10,000 – 100,000 streams/month
• UNDERGROUND: < 10,000 streams/month

The tier system prevents major labels from crowding out smaller bands. Voting pools are separated by tier.

fanVote.title: Fan Vote
fanVote.description: Quadratic Voting — 100 Voice Credits per month
fanVote.helpText:
Quadratic Voting lets you express intensity without overwhelming minorities.

The cost of n votes on the same track is n² credits:
• 1 vote = 1 credit
• 2 votes = 4 credits
• 3 votes = 9 credits
• 5 votes = 25 credits

You can spread your 100 credits broadly or bet intensely on one track — but extreme concentration gets expensive.
fanVote.confirmSubmit: Are you sure you want to submit these votes?

djVote.title: DJ Ballot
djVote.dragToRank: Drag tracks into your preferred order
djVote.helpTitle: Why drag & drop instead of points?
djVote.helpText:
The Schulze Method (Beatpath) finds the Condorcet winner: the track that wins all pairwise comparisons.

Rankings instead of points make strategic burial impossible:
• You cannot harm a competitor by tactically rating a rival low.
• The algorithm evaluates all pairwise comparisons and finds the strongest path.

Simply put: your honest order is always the best strategy.

peerVote.title: Band Peer Review
peerVote.helpTitle: How does the system detect voting rings?
peerVote.helpText:
Three security layers protect against coordinated manipulation:

1. Clique detection: Reciprocal votes (A→B and B→A) are downweighted.
2. Mahalanobis distance: Outlier voters are identified and weighted less.
3. Triadic Census: Closed triangles (A→B→C→A) receive a 0.7× penalty per triangle.

More intellectual diversity when voting (different genres, regions) increases the weight of your vote by up to 15%.

schulze.title: Schulze Method (Beatpath)
schulze.summary: DJs submit a ranking. The Condorcet winner wins.

peer.summary: Bands rate each other; voting rings are downweighted.

transparency.title: Algorithmic Transparency
transparency.subtitle: Full documentation of all vote-weighting steps

qv.title: Quadratic Voting (Fan Voting)
qv.description: Every fan receives 100 Voice Credits per month. The cost of n votes on the same track is n² credits. This quadratic cost function prevents individual fans from dominating results with concentrated credits.
qv.whyFair: Why does this prevent wealth concentration? Placing all credits on one track costs exponentially more — but marginal costs rise so steeply that spreading credits is the rational choice. Result: minority preferences carry more weight than in linear voting.

schulze.title: Schulze Method (DJ Voting)
schulze.description: DJs submit a complete ranking (drag & drop). The Schulze algorithm computes pairwise comparisons: for each track pair (A, B), it counts how many DJs ranked A above B.
schulze.beatpath: The strongest path (beatpath) between all pairs is computed with Floyd-Warshall. The track that wins all beatpath comparisons is the Condorcet winner.
schulze.burialProof: Burial-proof: Because rankings are used instead of point scores, no DJ can harm a competitor by tactically rating a rival low. The honest ranking is always the dominant strategy.

combined.title: Combined Charts (33/33/33)
combined.description: The Combined Charts aggregate the Fan Score, DJ Score and Peer Score with equal weights (33.3% each).
combined.normalization: Before combination, each score is Min-Max normalised: normScore = (score - min) / (max - min). This prevents a score with larger variance from dominating.

antiManipulation.title: Anti-Manipulation Algorithms
antiManipulation.cliqueDetection: Clique Detection: Reciprocal votes (A→B and B→A) are penalised. The more reciprocal votes in a group, the greater the downweight.
antiManipulation.triadicCensus: Triadic Census: Closed triangles (A→B→C→A) receive a 0.7× penalty per triangle. This algorithm detects voting rings that inflate each other's scores.
antiManipulation.mahalanobis: Mahalanobis Distance: Outlier voters are identified statistically. A voter whose voting pattern deviates more than 3 standard deviations from the field is weighted less.
antiManipulation.intellectualDistance: Intellectual Distance Bonus: Bands that vote across diverse genres and regions (high Shannon entropy) receive a weight bonus of up to 1.15×. This rewards genuine musical engagement.

tierSystem.title: Tier System
tierSystem.description: Tiers are based on monthly Spotify followers and define the competitive group and pricing for additional chart categories.
tierSystem.decoupling: Algorithmic decoupling: Financial contributions have ZERO influence on chart ranking scores. Payment only unlocks participation in additional categories — nothing more.

randomBand.title: Random Band of the Day
randomBand.description: A band from the Micro or Emerging tier is randomly selected every day. The algorithm uses a Linear Congruential Generator (LCG) seeded with the current date.
randomBand.seed: Seed = YYYY-MM-DD as an integer. This guarantees reproducibility: anyone can independently verify today's Band of the Day.
